observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a) Stop the practice of recording purchases of supplies and materials directly to the related expense account and, henceforth, record purchases and utilization of these items in accordance with Sections 114 and 121 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; b) Require the requisitioning office needing the supplies and materials to prepare and submit the RIS to the Property Custodian on a weekly basis; c) Require the Property Custodian to consolidate all the RIS on the SSMI and submit the same to the Accounting Office; and d) Require the Municipal Accountant to record to the related expense accounts the supplies consumed based on the SSMI and to maintain perpetual inventory records to be reconciled periodically with the records of the GSO in order to fairly present the expense and inventory accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: No personnel had been assigned to control and monitor the issuance of supplies recorded under inventory account. These supplies had already been utilized by the requisitioning office upon delivery.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the designated Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer and other concerned personnel to submit copies of distribution lists duly acknowledged by the recipients to validate actual distribution. We further recommended that the distribution lists should contain the name and signature of recipients as well as the date of receipt, and such other necessary information, including the kind and quantity of items received to facilitate accounting for the medical supplies and to establish accountability.
observations.managementAction: The concerned office was still consolidating the distribution list of the 14 barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management stop the practice of procurement through reimbursement and strictly observe the pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and relevant GPPB issuances to ensure transparency, economy and efficiency in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: The number of returnees was unpredictable, hence no definite number of persons to be served could be determined. Food provisions to the quarantined PUMs vary depending on their time of arrival, the number of persons, and individual needs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the personnel concerned or the end-user duly authorized by the Head of the PE to properly and completely fill up the relevant and required information in the POs to allow comparison with the prevailing market prices in the locality, or with the existing price data of the agency, the Department of Trade and Industry or other relevant agencies.
observations.managementAction: The BAC believed that specification as to the brand name is prohibited in RA 9184. They were not aware of the provision of COA Circular No. 96-010 which required the brand name in the PO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer submit documents to support payments of cash assistance to stranded individuals affected by the COVID-19 pandemic, such as an SB resolution establishing the guidelines and rates of financial assistance, authorization letters for claimants receiving payments in behalf of stranded individuals and proof of receipt by the latter.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and Accountant were still complying the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that the Contract of Agreement contains terms and conditions, such as the contract duration or time schedule, to ensure that projects are completed within the timeframe set by the Municipality and liquidated damages be imposed in case of any unjustifiable delay in accordance with Section 8, Annex “E” of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality used a pro-forma Contract of Agreement and overlooked that the contract period was not indicated in the terms and conditions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to prepare the PMR and submit the same to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within 14 calendar days after the end of each semester in accordance with Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC had not prepared the Procurement Monitoring Reports due to the voluminous workload.

observations.recommendation: Consider the filling up of the vacant positions in the Local DRRM Office who will perform the mandated functions and responsibilities enumerated in NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 as soon as qualified applicants are available.
observations.managementAction: No applicants met the qualification standards on training and experience.

observations.recommendation: Required the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit immediately the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAF) to the auditor for verification.
observations.managementAction: The Collecting Officers did not prepare the updated RAAF, hence the Municipal Treasurer was not able to prepare the consolidated report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
(a) Require all concerned accountable officers to immediately settle their cash
advances in accordance with the pertinent sections of COA Circular No. 97-
002 dated February 10, 1997;
(b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to monitor and strictly enforce the
liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed period, and see to it that
no additional cash advances are granted unless the previous ones are settled
or properly accounted for; and
(c) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular
evaluation/validation of all outstanding accounts; send demand letters to
officers and employees with long outstanding accounts, and impose
sanctions when necessary, such as withholding of their salaries as provided
in the Circular and, if warranted, request for the write-off of long-overdue
cash advances in accordance with the procedures provided under COA
Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the concerned department heads strictly comply with the
accounting and reporting guidelines on the local roads asset management system, and
earnestly perform their respective duties and responsibilities set forth under COA
Circular No. 2015-008 dated October 23, 2015.

We also recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct an annual physical
count of all its Local Road Networks and the results thereof reported in the RPCLRN
to be submitted to the Auditor and Accounting Unit not later than January 31 of each
year. The RPCLRN shall be reconciled with the accounting records and the necessary
adjusting entries be prepared before the statement date.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee
to conduct a regular physical inventory of the property of the Municipality and
eventually prepare and submit the RPCPPE within the prescribed period as required
by regulations to allow periodic reconciliation of property records, and assure the
accuracy of reported PPE balances in the financial statements.

We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal
Accountant maintain property cards and ledgers cards, respectively, in accordance with
Sections 114 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I.

We recommended that Management submit a Summary/List of Donations
Received, Distributed and Balances supported with (a) Proof of receipt by and
distribution to the beneficiaries and (b) Inventory of remaining undistributed items, if
any, in accordance with Section 1 of COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020.

We recommended that the MDRRMO coordinate with the Office of Civil
Defense (OCD) in preparing and submitting the Municipality’s PSCP pursuant to
NDRRMC Memorandum No 33, s. 2018.

We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) institutionalize
the PSCP by creating a working group that will perform the following activities:
a. To conduct risk assessment;
b. To identify critical processes and functions;
c. To determine scenarios that made disrupt normal operation;
d. To conduct risk analysis and impact analysis;
e. To formulate the PSCP based on the identified risks on critical
processes and functions and its related steps to be followed to
eliminate, if not mitigate, the impact of the determined
disruptions following the prescribed templates and requirements
in the PSCP guidebook; and
f. To submit plan to the PDRRMO for review, to the Local Chief
Executive for approval, and to the Sangguniang Bayan for the
passage of a resolution to institutionalize the PSCP.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the concerned office
responsible for the distribution of welfare goods to submit copies of distribution list that
contain the name and signature of recipients as well as the date of receipt, and such
other necessary information, including the kind and quantity of items received to
facilitate accounting, thereof.

We further recommended that the approving officer/Head of the PE ensure that
POs are completely filled out with detailed specifications and other relevant information
to allow comparison with the prevailing market prices in the locality, or with the existing
price data of the agency, the Department of Trade and Industry or other relevant
agencies.

We recommended and Management agreed that Management submit a
breakdown of each caterer’s menu offering and a written explanation of how the
caterers who participated in the canvass could come up with their bids despite the lack
of a menu.

We further recommended that Management ensure that the officials involved in
the procurement process strictly comply with the procurement procedures through SVP
under Item 8.b, Annex H of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184, to protect the interest of
the government.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the contractors to post
warranty security in the amount provided under Section 62.2.3.3 of the Revised IRR of
RA 9184 in procuring infrastructure projects. Otherwise, structural defects and/or
failure within the warranty period not covered by the surety bond and not repaired by
the contractor shall be borne by the persons liable.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reclassify the
completed projects to the appropriate asset accounts and transfer these to the General
Fund as prescribed in the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to:
(a) Stop the practice of drawing cash advance for payroll over the net amount for the
pay period;
(b) Issue Official Receipt on the excess cash advance and deposit the same to the
depository bank;
(c) Use the safe to keep the cash and other accountabilities in her custody; and
(d) Submit an explanation why the amount of cash advances for salaries was more than
the net amount of payroll for the particular pay period.

We recommended that Management adhere to the requirements prescribed under DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 09, series of 2020, in the implementation of the SAP and other similar programs of DSWD to ensure that financial assistance is given to qualified beneficiaries and that the objectives of the program are attained.
observations.managementAction: The concerned officials had already conducted a thorough validation and assessment to identify the qualified cash assistance beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Development Council specifically describe the projects for inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan and always secure the approval or authorization of the Sangguniang Bayan before incurring expenditures and/or entering into a contract for the implementation of projects described in generic terms in the appropriation ordinance.
observations.managementAction: The Management had identified projects in the 20% Development Fund specifically indicated in the AIP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management stop giving educational assistance in the form of cash to solo parents. We further recommended that assistance extended to solo parents should be in accordance with the provisions of R.A. No. 8972.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had stopped the granting of cash as educational assistance to solo parents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enter into a contract with the winning bidder within ten calendar days from receipt by the winning bidder of the NOA in accordance with Section 37.2.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, to avoid delay in the implementation of projects.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor had observed the allowable timeline within which to enter into a contract with the winning bidders. The subject projects had been completed in March 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the suppliers to post-warranty security in the amount provided under Section 62.1 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to ensure that suppliers correct any manufacturing defects on delivered goods.
observations.managementAction: Upon payment, a warranty security equivalent of 1% of the total cost of the procured goods had been retained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor prioritize the issuance of the Executive Order on the composition of the Inventory Committee and direct the Committee to conduct the actual count of inventories and movable properties. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer as Property Custodian and the Municipal Accountant maintain stock/property cards and ledgers cards, respectively, in accordance with Sections 114 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor had already issued an executive order on the composition of the Inventory Committee. The committee had started the conduct of physical count of PPEs by the office.
observations.reason: There was a difficulty in identifying the PPEs acquired in the previous years due to the absence of property records. The Property Custodian was not able to maintain property records of PPEs due to a lack of personnel to handle the task.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the BAC to submit the basis for the price of the PPEs and other medical supplies as well as an explanation of the material price difference. We further recommended that for the Municipality’s procurement activities, the BAC conduct preliminary market scanning to get the prevailing market price or make use of the existing price data of the DOH or other relevant agencies to ensure that the government get the most advantageous price.
observations.managementAction: The BAC adhered to the policy on RA 9184 on subsequent purchases.
observations.reason: The BAC had a difficulty finding a possible supplier in and out of the province due to the risk of the COVID-19 pandemic. The committee was not aware of the Suggested Retail Price (SRP) posted by the DOH during the early stage of the pandemic, hence was not able to use it as a reference. The cost estimates had been based on the preliminary canvass made from the medical suppliers due to the urgent need for the medical supplies to safeguard the frontliners.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit an explanation why the required warranty clause was not indicated in the POs and OSS. We further recommended that Management ensure compliance with Section 3.5 of GPPB Circular 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020, otherwise, they will be held answerable for negligence in case of uncorrected defects on the delivered goods.
observations.managementAction: Warranty security of 1% of the total cost of goods was retained upon payment.
observations.reason: Retention of 1% of the total cost of goods should answer/cover any defect on the delivered goods.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require all concerned accountable officers to immediately liquidate their cash advances in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, otherwise, cause the withholding of salaries of those who are still active in the service. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant credit only the Advances to Officers and Employees account for the amount of cash advances granted and effect the necessary adjustments for the negative account balances. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct regular evaluation and validation of all outstanding accounts and if warranted, request for write-off of long-overdue cash advances in accordance with the procedures under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality no longer granted cash advances for travel expenses. Instead, these had been paid through reimbursements upon completion of the travel. Repayments of the outstanding cash advances were made on an installment basis through payroll deductions.
observations.reason: Some accountable officers were no longer connected with the LGU due to retirement, end of term, and death.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, Management adhere strictly to the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, particularly on the issuance of certification on the conduct of detailed engineering surveys and designs, and posting of the NOA in the PhilGEPS and in any conspicuous place reserved for the purpose.
observations.managementAction: The BAC had already observed the allowable timelines in their procurements. The NOA and NTP had been posted within the Municipality’s vicinity, specifically reserved for the purpose.
observations.reason: Posting of NOA and NTP in the PhilGEPS had not been made due to slow internet connectivity.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure timely completion of projects by directing the BAC to observe the provisions of the IRR particularly on post-qualification of contractors in terms of manpower and equipment requirements and designate personnel to monitor closely and supervise the implementation of projects in accordance with the terms and conditions in the Contract Agreement.
observations.managementAction: A job order worker had been hired to assist the Municipal Engineer in the monitoring of the implemented projects.
observations.reason: Due to other important assignments that needed immediate attention and a lack of manpower in the Engineering Office, the monitoring of projects was not done regularly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the provisions of NDRRMC, DBM, and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 dated March 25, 2013, on the utilization of the LDRRMF. We further recommend that Management stop the practice of charging against the LDRRMF the wages of JO personnel performing activities, not in line with those set forth in the regulations, but rather charge the same to the regular Maintenance and Other Operation Expenses under the General Fund Proper.
observations.managementAction: JO workers had been hired to perform COVID-19 related activities charged to LDRRMF.
observations.reason: The appropriations in the General Fund were not sufficient for wages of the JO workers assigned in monitoring and providing food to the quarantined LSIs and OFWs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 by requiring the prompt submission of copies of perfected contracts with its supporting documents and Purchase Order for timely auditorial review and technical evaluation and so the management may be also informed on deficiencies noted, if any.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat had already submitted copies of the contracts of the infrastructure projects.
observations.reason: There was still a delay in the submission of the supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to initiate the return of the unutilized balances of the CDF and PDAF to the National Treasury through the Bureau of Treasury in compliance with the Supreme Court Decision declaring such releases as unconstitutional.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer had inquired the bank about the account name and account number to which the unexpended balances would be deposited; however, they were referred to the Bureau of Treasury in Dumaguete City.
observations.reason: There was a difficulty in determining the bank account name and account number.

observations.recommendation: Update the preparation and submission of the subject bank reconciliation statements. Henceforth, strictly observe the mandated timeline for the submission of the same report.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had exerted effort to submit the bank reconciliation statements (BRS). However, the BRS for all funds for the period January to December 2021 were submitted only in May 2022.
observations.reason: There was a lack of personnel in the Accounting Office to focus on the preparation, hence, the submission of the monthly BRS was not done within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: Appropriate funds for the titling of lots to establish ownership thereof and protect the interest of the government from other interested parties.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer had already coordinated with the Municipal Assessor and other national government agencies for the necessary documents in the titling of the lots.
observations.reason: Of the 12 parcels of land, only four have Certificates of Title.

observations.recommendation: Observe strictly the allowable period in the award and implementation of the contract as prescribed under the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulation of RA 9184 in order to achieve timely completion of the project.
observations.managementAction: The BAC had already observed the allowable timelines in their subsequent procurements.
observations.reason: The volume of work and other functions performed by BAC members had caused the delay in the procurement process.

observations.recommendation: Required the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Report on Sources and utilization of DRRMF using the prescribed format on a monthly basis and require the LDRRMO to submit such report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the COA Auditor.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had prepared and submitted the Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to the DILG.
observations.reason: The Monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF for CY 2020 had not been furnished to the Auditor. This was overlooked by the Municipal Accountant due to the volume of work and lack of personnel.

observations.recommendation: Allocate 1% of its Internal Revenue Allotment for strengthening and implementation of programs, projects, and activities for the protection and welfare of children as provided under RA 9344.
observations.managementAction: The LCPC-related programs and activities had been incorporated into the appropriations of the different offices in the Municipality.
observations.reason: The Municipality had limited funds for the 1% LCPC allocation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the fund transfer under the “Due from LGUs” account and require the concerned barangays to submit the liquidation reports to validate the regularity and propriety of the expenses incurred. Further, require the return of any unutilized/unexpended balance of the fund transfer.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had recorded the fund transfers in accordance with PPSAS. The recipient barangays had been required to submit a liquidation report on funds received from the Municipality.
observations.reason: The concerned barangays were not aware of the preparation of a liquidation report for funds received from the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: Directed the Municipal Accountant to comply strictly with the provision of Section 6.05 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 on the timely submission of the accounts. Otherwise, impose administrative disciplinary action under Section 122 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had exerted effort to submit the financial reports on time. The year-end financial reports were submitted to the Auditor on April 22, 2022.
observations.reason: There was a lack of personnel to assist the Municipal Accountant in the preparation of financial reports.

observations.recommendation: Create an appraisal committee to appraise the value of the unserviceable properties classified under the Other Assets account and initiate steps for the proper disposal of these assets as provided under Section 502 of the GAAM, Volume I to protect the assets of the Municipality including the pecuniary interests/benefits that may accrue thereto.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had organized the Appraisal Committee.
observations.reason: The Committee had not yet conducted an appraisal of the unserviceable properties which shall be the basis for the disposal.

observations.recommendation: Directed all those who received the PEI to refund the amount of P10,000.00 each, the payment of which is not legally due them, the PS limitation provided under Section 325(a) of RA 7160, having been not observed by the municipality. Henceforth, strictly observe the fifty-five percent (55%) limitation on personal services to prevent disallowances in audit.
observations.managementAction: Partial refunds of the disallowance had been made through payroll deduction.
observations.reason: The employees could not afford to pay in full.

observations.recommendation: Required all persons held liable to immediately settle in full, the disallowed amount as required under COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 to enable the Municipality to utilize the funds for its income generating or priority development projects.
observations.managementAction: Refunds of the disallowed amount had been made through a monthly payroll deduction.
observations.reason: The net take home pay of most employees were not enough to pay the disallowed amount in full.

observations.recommendation: Exhaust all legal remedies for the collection of the accounts with debtors who are still existing or can still be located/identified.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer had sent demand letters to the known borrowers. The Municipal Accountant had reviewed the documents for a possible write-off, especially those uncollectible.
observations.reason: Some of the borrowers could no longer be located, while some were already dead and retired from the service. There was an absence of supporting documents for previous years’ receivables.

observations.recommendation: Required the concerned municipal officials to adopt remedies to increase/improve collection on real property taxes. Also, require the Municipal Treasurer to initiate steps to intensify collections of delinquent accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer had sent demand letters to delinquent taxpayers.
observations.reason: The concerned taxpayers failed to pay due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: Required the concerned municipal officials to adopt strategies and activities that ensure proper waste generation, efficient waste collection and segregation, disposal and recycling. Likewise, conduct information drive to make the public aware of their responsibility for waste reduction, reuse and/or recycle.
observations.managementAction: An information drive had been conducted on proper waste segregation in the barangays. Each barangay was required to have a Material Recovery Facility.
observations.reason: The Municipality still had no available lot for a sanitary landfill.

observations.recommendation: Required the concerned municipal officials to establish steps and strategies to improve the collection on long over-due loans. In addition, advise the officials of the DSWD to monitor the utilization of the loan availed from the municipality to ensure that this will be used in income-generating.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer had already sent collection letters to the concerned borrowers.
observations.reason: Some of the borrowers failed to pay; the DSWD SEA-K Program assisted some borrowers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the actual count of inventories and movable properties, and the Municipal Treasurer as Property Custodian and the Municipal Accountant to maintain stock/property cards and ledgers cards, respectively, in accordance with Sections 114 and 124 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: There was reorganization on the composition of the Inventory Committee. The Municipal Mayor will issue a revise Executive Order.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that the Contract of Agreement contains terms and conditions, such as the contract duration or time schedule, to ensure that projects are completed within the timeframe set by Municipality and liquidated damages be imposed in case of any unjustifiable delay in accordance with Section 8, Annex “E” of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is using a pro-forma Contract of Agreement and was not aware that the contract period was not indicated as one of the terms and conditions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to prepare the PMR and submit the same to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within 14 calendar days after the end of each semester in accordance with Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Overlooked by the BAC Secretariat due to the volume of work in their respective offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the suppliers to post warranty security in the amount provided under Section 62.1 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to ensure that suppliers correct any manufacturing defects on delivered goods.
observations.managementAction: The imposition of 1% warranty security was overlooked upon payment of procured goods.

observations.recommendation: Consider the filling up of the vacant positions in the Local DRRM Office who will perform the mandated functions and responsibilities enumerated in NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 as soon as qualified applicants are available.
observations.managementAction: No applicants met the qualification standards on training and experience.

observations.recommendation: Required the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit immediately the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAF) to the auditor for verification.
observations.managementAction: The Collecting Officers did not prepare the updated RAAF, hence, the Municipal Treasurer was not able to prepare the consolidated report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor prioritize the issuance of the
Executive Order on the composition of the Inventory Committee and direct the
Committee to conduct the actual count of inventories and movable properties. Likewise,
we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer as Property Custodian and the
Municipal Accountant maintain stock/property cards and ledgers cards, respectively,
in accordance with Sections 114 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I.

We recommended that Management:
a) Stop the practice of recording purchases of supplies and materials directly to the
related expense account and, henceforth, record purchases and utilization of
these items in accordance with Sections 114 and 121 of the Manual on NGAS for
LGUs, Volume I;
b) Require the requisitioning office needing the supplies and materials to prepare
and submit the RIS to the Property Custodian on a weekly basis;
c) Require the Property Custodian to consolidate all the RIS on the SSMI and
submit the same to the Accounting Office; and
d) Require the Municipal Accountant to record to the related expense accounts the
supplies consumed based on the SSMI and to maintain perpetual inventory
records to be reconciled periodically with the records of the GSO in order to
fairly present the expense and inventory accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of procurement through
reimbursement and strictly observe the pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR of RA
No. 9184 and relevant GPPB issuances to ensure transparency, economy and efficiency
in the procurement process.

We recommended that Management require the BAC to submit the basis for the price of
the PPEs and other medical supplies as well as an explanation on the material price
difference. We further recommended that for the Municipality’s procurement
activities, the BAC conduct preliminary market scanning to get the prevailing market
price or make use of the existing price data of the DOH or other relevant agencies to
ensure that the government get the most advantageous price.

We recommended that Management require the designated Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer and other concerned personnel to submit copies of
distribution lists duly acknowledged by the recipients to validate actual distribution. We
further recommended that the distribution lists should contain the name and signature of
recipients as well as the date of receipt, and such other necessary information, including
the kind and quantity of items received to facilitate accounting for the medical supplies
and to establish accountability.

We recommend that Management require the personnel concerned or the end-
user duly authorized by the Head of the PE to properly and completely fill up the
relevant and required information in the POs to allow comparison with the prevailing
market prices in the locality, or with the existing price data of the agency, the
Department of Trade and Industry or other relevant agencies.

We recommended that Management submit an explanation why the required
warranty clause was not indicated in the POs and OSS. We further recommended that
Management ensure compliance with Section 3.5 of GPPB Circular 01-2020 dated April
6, 2020, otherwise, they will be held answerable for negligence in case of uncorrected
defects on the delivered goods.

We recommended that Management adhere to the requirements prescribed
under DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 09, series of 2020, in the implementation of
the SAP and other similar programs of DSWD to ensure that financial assistance are
given to qualified beneficiaries and the objectives of the program are attained.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer submit
documents to support payments of cash assistance to stranded individuals affected by
the COVID-19 pandemic, such as an SB resolution establishing the guidelines and rates
of financial assistance, authorization letters for claimants receiving payments in behalf
of stranded individuals and proof of receipt by the latter.

We recommended that the Municipal Development Council specifically describe the
projects for inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan and always secure the approval
or authorization of the Sangguniang Bayan before incurring expenditures and/or
entering into contract for the implementation of projects described in generic terms in
the appropriation ordinance.

We recommended that Management stop giving educational assistance in the form of
cash to solo parents. We further recommended that assistance extended to solo parents
should be in accordance with the provisions of R.A. No. 8972.

We recommended that Management issue the NTP to the winning bidder within seven calendar days from the date of approval of the contract in accordance with Section 37.4.1 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Issuance of NTP on subsequent projects was made within the allowable prescribed period under the Revised IRR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer(a) record the RCDs daily and chronologically in the cashbooks; (b) deposit her collections intact daily or on the next banking day; (c) use the safe to keep her collections and other accountabilities; and (d) request Management to properly secure her Office from unauthorized persons by enclosing it.
observations.managementAction:
Deposit and recording of collections were made daily.
A fully enclosed office for the Treasurer is provided in the newly completed building.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the revenue collectors to turn over their collections to the Municipal Treasurer at the close of every business day as required under the Manual, in order to protect government funds from loss or misappropriation. We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, henceforth, demand strict compliance by revenue collectors with the provision on the turnover of collections.
observations.managementAction: Revenue collectors were instructed to remit their collections daily to the Municipal Treasurer for deposit to the bank on the following day.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant process cash advance vouchers for the payment of RATA on the 17th day of each month to ensure that the amount of RATA granted to the municipal officials is based on the number of days of actual work performance on their workdays.
observations.managementAction: Cash advance for the payment of RATA was made on the 17th day of each month.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity direct the Bids and Awards Committee to conduct a thorough eligibility check, bid evaluation and post-qualification of bidders in the procurement of infrastructure projects in accordance with the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184. Henceforth, always see to it that all procurement of contracts through public bidding shall be advantageous to the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The BAC considered the Net Financial Contracting Capacity (NFCC) of a contractor in the evaluation of its eligibility.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned officials responsible in the procurement of infrastructure projects to strictly observe all stages in the award and implementation thereof as prescribed under the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulation of RA 9184 in order to establish the propriety/regularity of the procurement activities.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor observed the allowable timeline on infrastructure projects implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (MDRRMC) to decide on projects and programs to be charged to the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in the Trust Fund so that these be included in the LDRRMFIP for CY 2018 and thereafter pursuant to Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012- 002 in order to maximize the support of disaster risk reduction and management activities in the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Unexpended balances of LDRRMF were appropriated for activities to respond to COVID - 19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) and submit it to the Municipal Accountant, together with the DVs and supporting documents, for preparation of JEV and recording in the Check Disbursement Journal.
observations.managementAction: The RCI was prepared and submitted to the Accounting Office together with the DVs and supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require all concerned accountable officers to immediately liquidate their cash advances in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, otherwise, cause the withholding of salaries of those who are still active in the service. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant credit only to the Advances to Officers and Employees account the amount of cash advances granted and effect the necessary adjustments for the negative account balances. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct regular evaluation and validation of all outstanding account and if warranted, request for write-off of long-overdue cash advances in accordance with the procedures under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant did not grant cash advance for travelling expenses. Travelling expenses were made outstanding cash advance reimbursements upon completion of travel.
observations.reason:
Some accountable officers are no longer connected the LGU.
As of December 31, 2020, outstanding cash advance amounted to P248,649.64.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, Management adhere strictly with the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, particularly on the issuance of certification on the conduct of detailed engineering surveys and designs, and posting of the NOA in the PhilGEPS and in any conspicuous place reserved for the purpose.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer conducted detailed engineering surveys and designs on infrastructure projects. The NOA and NTP were posted in the vicinity of the Municipality reserved for the purpose.
observations.reason: Posting of NOA and NTP in the PhilGEPS was not made due to slow internet connectivity.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enter into contract with the winning bidder within ten calendar days from receipt by the winning bidder of the NOA in accordance with Section 37.2.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, to avoid delay in the implementation of projects.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor observed the allowable timeline within which to enter into contract with the winning bidders. Delays happened because some winning bidders did not come within the period for the contract signing. The subject projects were completed in March 2020.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel to coordinate with the winning bidders.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure timely completion of projects by directing the BAC to observe the provisions of the IRR particularly on post-qualification of contractors in terms of manpower and equipment requirement and designate a personnel to monitor closely and supervise the implementation of projects in accordance with the terms and conditions in the Contract Agreement.
observations.managementAction: A job order worker was hired to assist the Municipal Engineer in the monitoring of implemented projects.
observations.reason:
Due to other important assignments that also needed immediate attention and lack of manpower in the Engineering Office, monitoring of projects cannot be done regularly.
As of December 31, 2020, four projects with contract costs totaling P9,312,462.80 were partially completed as of target date of completion.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the provisions of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 dated March 25, 2013 on the utilization of the LDRRMF. We further recommend that Management stop the practice of charging against the LDRRMF the wages of JO personnel performing activities not in line with those set forth in the regulations, but rather charge the same to the regular Maintenance and Other Operation Expenses under the General Fund Proper.
observations.managementAction: JO workers hired to perform COVID-19 related activities were charged to LDRRMF.
observations.reason: Appropriations in the General Fund were not sufficient for wages of JO workers assigned in monitoring and providing food to quarantined LSIs and OFWs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 by requiring the prompt submission of copies of perfected contracts with its supporting documents and Purchase Order for timely auditorial review and technical evaluation and so the management may be also informed on deficiencies noted, if any.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat submitted copies of contracts for supporting documents.
observations.reason: Some delays still occurred in the submission of supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to initiate the return of the unutilized balances of the CDF and PDAF to the National Treasury through the Bureau of Treasury in compliance with the Supreme Court Decision declaring such releases as unconstitutional.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer had inquired from the bank for the account name and number for the return of the unexpended balances but was referred to the Bureau of Treasury in Dumaguete.
observations.reason: Information/data on the bank account name and number are not available.

observations.recommendation: Update the preparation and submission of the subject bank reconciliation statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the bank reconciliation statements. However, the BRS for January to December 2020 were submitted only in May 2021.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel in the Accounting Office, hence, the preparation and submission of the monthly bank reconciliation statements could not be done within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: Appropriate funds for the titling of the land to establish ownership thereof and protect the interest of the government from other interested parties.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer coordinated with Municipal Assessor and other national government agencies for the necessary documents in the titling of the lots.
Of the 12 parcels of land, only four have Certificates of Title.
observations.reason: Expenses for the titling of lots were not included in the CY 2020 budget.

observations.recommendation: Observe strictly the allowable period in the award and implementation of contract as prescribed under the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulation of RA 9184 in order to achieve timely completion of the project.
observations.managementAction: The BAC observed the allowable timelines in their subsequent procurements.
observations.reason:
The project was already completed but beyond the completion date.
The volume of work and other functions performed by BAC members caused the delay in the procurement process.

observations.recommendation: Required the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Report on Sources and utilization of DRRMF using the prescribed format on a monthly basis and require the LDRRMO to submit such report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the COA Auditor.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared and submitted to the DILG the Report on Sources and utilization of LDRRMF quarterly. This was overlooked by the Municipal Accountant due to the volume of work in his office and limited personnel.
observations.reason: The Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF for CY 2020 were not furnished to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: Allocate 1% of its Internal Revenue Allotment for strengthening and implementation of programs, projects and activities for the protection and welfare of children as provided under RA 9344.
observations.managementAction: Programs and activities related to LCPC were incorporated in the appropriations of the different offices in the Municipality.
observations.reason: The Municipality had limited funds for the allocation of 1% for LCPC.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record the fund transfer under the “Due from LGUs” account and require the concerned barangays to submit the liquidation reports to validate the regularity and propriety of the expenses incurred. Further, require the return of any unutilized/unexpended balance of the fund transfer.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant recorded the fund transfers in accordance with PPSAS. The recipient barangays were required to submit liquidation report on funds received from the Municipality.
observations.reason: The concerned barangays were not aware that liquidation report will be prepared for funds received from the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: Directed the Municipal Accountant to comply strictly with the provision of Section 6.05 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 on the timely submission of the accounts. Otherwise, impose administrative disciplinary action under Section 122 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant exerted effort to submit the financial reports on time.
Year-end financial reports were submitted to the Auditor on March 25, 2021.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel to assist the Municipal Accountant in the preparation of financial reports.

observations.recommendation: Create an appraisal committee to appraise the value of the unserviceable properties classified under the Other Assets account and initiate steps for the proper disposal of these assets as provided under Section 502 of the GAAM, Volume I to protect the assets of the Municipality including the pecuniary interests/benefits that may accrue thereto.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had organized the Appraisal Committee.
observations.reason: The Committee had not yet conducted appraisal of unserviceable properties which shall be the basis for the disposal.

observations.recommendation: Directed all those who received the PEI to refund the amount of P10,000.00 each, the payment of which is not legally due them, the PS limitation provided under Section 325(a) of RA 7160, having been not observed by the municipality. Henceforth, strictly observe the fifty-five percent (55%) limitation on personal services to prevent disallowances in audit.
observations.managementAction: Partial refunds of the disallowance were made through payroll deduction.
observations.reason: Employees could not afford to pay in full.

observations.recommendation: Required all persons held liable to immediately settle in full, the disallowed amount as required under COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 to enable the Municipality to utilize the funds for its income generating or priority development projects.
observations.managementAction: Refunds of the disallowed amount were made through monthly payroll deduction.
observations.reason: The net take home pay of most employees was not enough to pay the disallowed amount in full.

observations.recommendation: Exhaust all legal remedies for the collection of the accounts with debtors who are still existing or can still be located/identified.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer sent demand letters to known borrowers. The Municipal Accountant had reviewed the receivables account for possible write-off.
observations.reason:
Some of the borrowers could no longer be located and some were already dead and retired from the service.
Absence of documents to support the receivables account of previous years.

observations.recommendation: Required the concerned municipal officials to adopt remedies to increase/improve collection on real property taxes. Also, require the Municipal Treasurer to initiate steps to intensify collections of delinquent accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer sent demand letters to delinquent taxpayers.
observations.reason: Failure of the concerned taxpayers to pay due to COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: Required the concerned municipal officials to adopt strategies and activities that ensure proper waste generation, efficient waste collection and segregation, disposal and recycling. Likewise, conduct information drive to make the public aware of their responsibility for waste reduction, reuse and/or recycle.
observations.managementAction: Information drive had been conducted on proper waste segregation in the barangays. The Municipality had constructed Material Recovery Facility in the newly acquired lot. Each barangay was required to have a Material Recovery Facility.
observations.reason: The Municipality has no available lot for a sanitary landfill.

observations.recommendation: Required the concerned municipal officials to establish steps and strategies to improve the collection on long over-due loans. In addition, advise the officials of the DSWD to monitor the utilization of the loan availed from the municipality to ensure that this will be used in income-generating.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer sent collection letters to concerned borrowers.
observations.reason: Due to financial constraint the concerned borrowers under the DSWS SEA-K Program could not pay their loan.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require all concerned accountable
officers to immediately liquidate their cash advances in accordance with COA
Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, otherwise, cause the withholding of
salaries of those who are still active in the service. We also recommended that the
Municipal Accountant credit only to the Advances to Officers and Employees account
the amount of cash advances granted and effect the necessary adjustments for the
negative account balances. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant
conduct regular evaluation and validation of all outstanding account and if
warranted, request for write-off of long-overdue cash advances in accordance with
theprocedures underCOA CircularNo. 2016-005 dated December19,2016.

We recommended that Management direct the Inventory Committee to
conduct the actual count of inventories and movable properties, and the Municipal
Treasurer as Property Custodian and the Municipal Accountant to maintain
stock/property cards and ledgers cards, respectively, in accordance with Sections 114
and124oftheManual on theNGAS, Volume I

We recommended that, henceforth, Management adhere strictly with the
provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, particularly on the issuance of
certification on the conduct of detailed engineering surveys and designs, and posting
oftheNOA inthePhilGEPS andinany conspicuous placereserved forthepurpose.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enter into contract with the
winning bidder within ten calendar days from receipt by the winning bidder of the
NOA in accordance with Section 37.2.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, to avoid
delayin theimplementation of projects.

We recommended that Management issue the NTP to the winning bidder
within seven calendar days from the date of approval of the contract in accordance
with Section 37.4.1oftheRevised IRRofRA No.9184.

We recommended that Management ensure timely completion of projects by
directing the BAC to observe the provisions of the IRR particularly on post-
qualification of contractors in terms of manpower and equipment requirement and
designate a personnel to monitor closely and supervise the implementation of projects
inaccordancewith the terms andconditionsin theContract Agreement.

We recommended that Management ensure that the Contract of Agreement
contains terms and conditions, such as the contract duration or time schedule, to
ensure that projects are completed within the timeframe set by Municipality and
liquidated damages be imposed in case of any unjustifiable delay in accordance with
Section 8,Annex “E”of theRevised IRR ofRANo. 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to prepare the
PMR and submit the same to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within 14
calendar days after the end of each semester in accordance with Section 12.2 of the
Revised IRRof R.A. 9184.

We recommended that Management require the suppliers to post warranty
security in the amount provided under Section 62.1 of the Revised IRR of RA No.
9184toensurethat supplierscorrect anymanufacturing defects ondelivered goods.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the provisions of
NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 dated March
25, 2013 on the utilization of the LDRRMF. We further recommend that
Management stop the practice of charging against the LDRRMF the wages of JO
personnel performing activities not in line with those set forth in the regulations, but
rather charge the same to the regular Maintenance and Other Operation Expenses
underthe General FundProper.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer(a) record the RCDs daily and
chronologically in the cashbooks; (b) deposit her collections intact daily or on the next
banking day; (c) use the safe to keep her collections and other accountabilities; and (d)
request Management to properly secure her Office from unauthorized persons by
enclosingit.

We recommended that Management direct the revenue collectors to turn over
their collections to the Municipal Treasurer at the close of every business day as
required under the Manual, in order to protect government funds from loss or
misappropriation. We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer,
henceforth, demand strict compliance by revenue collectors with the provision on the
turnoverof collections.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant process cash advance
vouchers for the payment of RATA on the 17th day of each month to ensure that the
amount of RATA granted to the municipal officials is based on the number of days
ofactual workperformance on theirworkdays.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to trace the causes for the difference of the reciprocal accounts and effect adjustments in order to present reliable data on the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to trace the causes for the difference of the reciprocal accounts and effect adjustments in order to present reliable data on the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant require specific approval/authority from the Local Sanggunianin in the implementation of specific projects and activities in the 20% Development Fund  which are not specified in the appropriation ordinance.

We recommended that Management issue a Warning Letter to concerned Contractors and require the submission of a Catch-up Plan to complete the works within the approved contract period pursuant to Item VI.A.3.e of the DILG Memorandum Circular (MC) No. 68 Series of 20014 dated June 3, 2014.  We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to continue monitor the implementation of projects and see to it that these are completed within the target date/s, ensuring that the needed infrastructure facilities are provided to the constituents at the earliest possible time.

We recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity direct the Bids and Awards Committee to conduct a thorough eligibility check, bid evaluation and post-qualification of bidders in the procurement of infrastructure projects in accordance with the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.  Henceforth, always see to it that all procurement of contracts through public bidding shall be advantageous to the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned officials responsible in the procurement of infrastructure projects to strictly observe all stages in the award and implementation thereof as prescribed under the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulation of RA 9184 in order to establish the propriety/regularity of the procurement activities.

We recommended that management strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 by requiring the prompt submission of copies of perfected contracts with its supporting documents and Purchase Order for timely auditorial review and technical evaluation and so the management may be also informed on deficiencies noted, if any.

We recommended that extension of contract time shall be supported with a justifiable circumstances duly investigated and/or evaluated by the procuring entity,  otherwise, unjustified extension cannot be granted and any delayed in  completion be subjected with  liquidated damages upon final payment.

We recommended that Management require the suppliers to post Warranty Security in the amount provided under Section 62.1 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 in the procurement of goods in order to fully protect the interest of the procuring agency  in case of manufacturing defects on the delivered goods.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to initiate the return of the unutilized balances of the CDF and PDAF to the National Treasury through the Bureau of Treasury in compliance with the Supreme Court Decision declaring such releases as unconstitutional.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned employee in-charge in the utilization of fuel, oil and lubricant to prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption as prescribed under COA Circular No. 75-6 in order to determine the efficiency and reasonableness of the expenditures.
Release the 25% barangay share from Real Property Tax collections in accordance with Section 271 (d) of RA 7160, so that this can be programmed by concerned barangays for priority projects.
observations.managementAction: Barangay shares were released to concerned barangays on a quarterly basis.

Consequently,  the Municipal Treasurer  is required to send demand letters to those in the list for the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances. If no liquidation is effected, impose the sanction of withholding the salaries of those who still fail to settle their accounts and consider the filing of appropriate charges against those who refuse to settle their accounts.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal  Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer facilitate  the transfer of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in the General Fund to a special Trust Fund  account in the Trust Fund to ensure that the funds are utilized solely to support disaster risk reduction and management activities.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant set up the Special Tax Receivable at the beginning of the year as provided in Section 84 of the NGAS for Local Government Units.  Further, we recommend that the  Municipal  Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant with the list of taxpayers with their corresponding tax due and collectible as basis in setting up the receivable account.
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer consider terminating the time deposit account of LDRRMF so that this can be readily utilized  to support disaster risk management activities.

We recommend that the Municipal mayor instruct the concerned officials to furnish the auditor with copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders within the prescribed period to facilitate auditorial review and evaluation of the terms and conditions thereof for Management’s corrective action.  In addition, we recommend that Purchase Order be duly filled up with all the data needed as guide for the supplier in the delivery of goods and for the Municipality to impose liquidated damages in case of delay in delivery of goods.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor consider the filling up of the vacant positions in the Local DRRM Office who will perform the mandated functions and responsibilities enumerated in NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 as soon as qualified applicants are available.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) and submit it to the Municipal Accountant, together with the DVs and supporting documents, for preparation of JEV and recording in the Check Disbursement Journal.
Limit the utilization of LDRRMF to the purposes allowed by law or regulations.

observations.reason: Verification of the records of the municipality showed that expenditures not related to LDRM were charged to General Fund under AICS.

observations.recommendation: Prioritize the acquisition of a lot fit for the establishment of a Material Recovery Facility so that programs, projects and activities identified in the LGU’s  Solid Waste Management Plan can be readily implemented.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal  Accountant to record the fund transfer under the “Due from LGUs” account and require the concerned barangays to submit the liquidation reports to validate the regularity and propriety of the expenses incurred. observations.recommendation: Require all concerned accountable officers to immediately settle/liquidate their cash advances otherwise, consider the withholding of salary pursuant to Sec. 5.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

observations.recommendation: Direct   Municipal Accountant comply strictly with the provision of Section 6.05 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated  May 18, 1995 on the timely submission of the accounts.
